## [00:00 - 03:01]

So, we're going to go over essentially how we spend up to $100,000 a day on YouTube ads. So who am I? I've been running paid advertising full time since 2010. I actually dropped out of college to pretty much go into internet marketing full time. I went to college to go play baseball and hated it, hated school, like playing baseball, but did not like doing classwork. And then I really hit it big with Facebook ads. 2010 made my first million dollars by the time I was 21 off of Facebook ads and fan pages and all of that. And you know, I've been running paid advertising since then. I'm the CEO and founder of Klein Accelerators, paid advertising agency. And we currently spend a little bit over $3 million a month across Facebook, YouTube, Google Display, Search, TikTok, and more. So here's why I believe YouTube ads are one of the greatest opportunities online right now. And the reason is the scale longevity and just control of YouTube is really unmatched across everything. Like we've scaled so many clients just way further on YouTube this year compared to Facebook. And the biggest reason is you can scale singular campaigns up to 40 to $50,000 a day and have them consistently hit KPI day after day for months. If any of you guys obviously run Facebook ads, you will see campaigns will die out and not last as long. So we've had winning creatives that have lasted for up to 16 months at over $9 million in spend. We actually have a creative right now that I'm going to go over that we started running in about, it was like December 2018. And we're still running it because we go back and forth between this client's two offers. And then the final thing is Google gives you a lot more control over working campaigns where you can simply cut out low performing demographics such as income, male, female, married, not married, age, and economic growth. Age, and et cetera. So with Google, you can actually go into campaigns that you're running right now. And let's say that you have an offer that's $10,000 and you see that, hey, the lead quality isn't that good. You can literally go into Google and turn off, let's say, unknown and the bottom 50%

income earners. And it's not like you have to go out and duplicate the campaign or change anything. As anyone who's run ads on Facebook for any significant time is seen, you're going to get ad accounts disabled. It doesn't matter how compliant you are. You could be running lead gen. We run a ton of stuff in the business opportunity space, internet marketing stuff, stuff that would get shut down immediately on Facebook. It doesn't work and they say YouTube ads just doesn't, you know, is not successful. And that's probably because they're not scripting their videos right and they're not testing enough. And then the other thing is you need large audience targeting. If you want to scale hard, if you want to scale up to the, you know, 50 to $100,000 a day mark, you need to go big on YouTube. So you need larger audiences to go after. Small placements and keywords just won't get you to the scale that you need. And then finally, you need the correct tracking and data. You know, the right tracking and analytics softwares can make or break you on YouTube. What a lot of people don't realize is Google is going to be off from anywhere from 30 to 40% based upon their tracking. There's just way too many moving parts now where people are changing their device, changing their browser, changing their email. And then finally you can have an intrigue angle, which is actually it's a little bit of a blinder angle and it's shorter. It's 45 seconds to one minute. You're pre-selling them on some amazing new discovery about your product or service. And usually this is going to go to a longer VSL or a longer text sales letter. So we actually have a client. He's, he might be on this this call. He's from this group. Absolutely crushing it with this angle. So when we do an initial rollout for a client, this is how we actually like to break out the budget. We really like in market audiences. So we usually give 50% of the budget to that. Then we do 20% of the budget to keyword campaigns, 20% of the budget to custom intent, and then 10% of the budget to remarketing. And then we use maximize conversions if your ads account doesn't have any data. So if you're on a brand new Google ads account, you, we really like to use maximize conversions and then switch over to target CPA. So when you're doing your initial campaign setups, your, your, your initial campaign setups, these are the campaign settings that you want to choose. So for new accounts, we choose

## [39:29 - 42:35]

leads or sales and we optimize for maximize conversions, like I was saying, and you want to choose YouTube videos under the network section. We don't want them showing up in the search results and we don't want them showing up on display network. Just choose YouTube videos. You want the pre roll and that's it. And then we split up our ad groups into desktop, mobile and tablet. So you'll have three different ad groups and we're testing three to four video creatives for across each ad group. Okay. So you're going to have essentially three ad groups with three to four creatives across all of them, the same exact video creatives. Now you're going to have essentially three types of bidding. So like I said, you're going to have maximize conversions. So we're going to roll this out for brand new campaigns to start to get data and feed Google's algorithm. So after 50 to 75 conversions, we're going to launch target CPA campaigns 90% of the time. The next type of bidding you have is cost per view. And we use this maybe three to 5% of the time. We don't really like it as it's not taking advantage of Google's algorithm. It generally does poorly. The best type of bidding that we found is target CPA. So after you get 50 to 75 conversions, you can literally tell Google what CPA you want to actually hit. And it will do its best to hit that or actually go below that. A lot of the times, let's say we're running, you know, a straight sale offer, we want conversions for 30 bucks, we can set the CPA at 30 bucks, and Google will start hitting, you know, 20, $20 CPAs. So a lot of the times you'll set the CPA and it will come in a lot lower than that. So let's go over in market campaigns. Google knows who is in the market for certain products and services, because these people, when you go into Google search and YouTube, and you're searching for things, it's going to put you into one of those categories. So let's just say you're actively researching, you know, search engine optimization, it's going to put you into advertising and marketing services, and now you're going to be able to target your YouTube ads towards that person. And these audiences do very, very well for us out of the gate for new accounts. These people are already in the market for it. So we roll these out first, and generally we give 50% of the budget to these types of campaigns. Then we get 20% of the budget to keyword campaigns, we use tubecif.com to find 75 to 150 relevant keywords for the niche. So when you're doing keyword campaigns,

## [42:35 - 45:39]

one of the things you have to realize, if you have 75 to 150 keywords, you have to optimize a little bit different when you're doing them. So you know, you're going to turn off keywords that are not doing well. So let's say you were doing lead gen, you're going to optimize based upon a rule of 3x CPA. So if you're looking for leads for $10, you're going to let a keyword run to $30 and spend. And if it's hitting the target CPA, you're going to keep it running. If not, you're going to pause it. Now, if you're doing a $47 to $97 product, you're not going to want to wait that long on a 3x target CPA for purchases, because you're going to blow your why you're going to spend way too much money. So you can move the optimization up the funnel, and you might set a let's say you want to get add to carts for, you know, 30 bucks. So that means that you're going to spend 3x the 30 you're going to spend up to $90 before you actually turn off that keyword. And you just have to do the math and map all that out to figure, figure all that out. Then you have your custom intent campaigns, custom intent campaigns, give us some of our largest scale. Okay, we have, we have campaigns for custom intent that we've spent millions upon millions of dollars on like a few million dollars on and just they hum along for five to $10,000 a day of spend, sometimes even harder than that $20, $30,000 a day, and they just keep going. So you're going to take 10 to 30 keywords and websites and build a custom intent audience in Google. So we kind of build out themes for each. So you might have a custom intent audience for affiliate marketing. So you'd have all your different keywords and websites that you'd want to throw in there. And that would be one custom intent audience, then you'd have, let's say search engine optimization, that would be another, and then you'd have, you know, how to make money online. And you can have all these different custom intent audiences. And then you could build a campaign around each theme. Then finally, you're going to have remarketing on YouTube ads. So you want to make sure that your YouTube channel is integrated into your Google ads account, make sure you set that up, and then set up these audiences. So you're going to have, you know, viewed any ad from a channel and each one of these, these recency segments is going to be its own audience. So you're going to have viewed any ad from a channel 30 days, 60 days, 90, 120, 180, 365, and 540. Then you can also do viewed any video from a channel. So if you actually have a channel that you're getting a lot of viewers on, this is going to be a very, very good audience. These people are already know, like and trust you, you

## [45:39 - 48:40]

can also build remarketing audiences on there. And then you're going to have your all website visitors, you know, 30, 60, 90, 120, all that. And you can launch each one of these as their own campaign with all of the different recency segments. So you would have a campaign where it would be remarketing, viewed any ad from a channel, and you would throw all of these different audiences into one campaign. And you can literally go in there into the audiences and turn off the ones that are not hitting the target CPA. So if you were just looking at it like this, it would be three campaigns. So we have a three X target CPA rule for ad groups that allow us to turn off things based upon hard data, and not emotion. So let's say if you're looking for sales at $30, you would let an ad group spend up to $90 before turning it off. So let's just say you had a campaign, it was in market, you know, they're in market for financial services, you have your desktop, your mobile, and your tablet ad groups, you're going to let each one of them spend up to $90. And if you're hitting your target CPA, let's say desktop is hitting, you know, $25 sales, mobiles hitting $20 sales, and tablet is hitting $60 sales, you would turn tablet out, tablet off, and you would let mobile and desktop continue to run. And you want to separate your ad groups into different device types, which allows you to look at data easier and make optimizations quicker. If something isn't doing well, we simply can turn it off. So you know, you're going to be able to see your desktop, mobile and tablet, literally in one in one view, and click a button to turn it off. The next thing you want to do when you're optimizing your campaigns is you want to go into the demographics, you want to check male versus female first, you'll usually have a big disparity there, you could either turn off male, or you could turn off female. Then finally, you're going to have your household income. So we run a lot of higher end offers, we run offers all the way up to $50,000 for consulting. And one of the first things we do for clients is shut off the lower 70% of income earners. If someone is coming in, they're watching a webinar, and you're pitching them something, you know, $5,000 to $10,000 over the phone, you need to have a high quality lead in that.

## [48:40 - 51:42]

So we literally just did this for a client, we took over their webinar, and they were actually, their lead to sale ratio was hovering around the 3% mark. In the past two weeks, their lead to sale ratio went to 5.3%. 5.3% just by shutting off the lower 70% of income earners. So you can get rid of a lot of the lower income earners if you are running a higher end offer, and you need people to be able to afford it. Then we go to age, you know, you're going to be able to look at, you know, 18 or 24, 25 to 34, and so on and so forth, and you can turn off ages that just aren't hitting your target KPI. So let's go over to how to scale budget from $500 a day to up to $50,000 a day. So here's the thing, Google's algorithm is extremely smart. So the more data you feed it, the easier it becomes to scale. So like we have accounts where, you know, we're feeding it thousands upon thousands of different conversions every single day. So you really want to focus on feeding the algorithm as much data as you can. We found Google's algorithm to be a lot better than Facebook's in optimizing. It gets better and better over time compared to Facebook where you launch a campaign and it's seen, you know, they get worse over time. Google gets better over time. So how we actually scale is we bump budgets 20% on a time and see if the CPA holds. So sometimes Google's algorithm takes some time to regulate new spends. So sometimes you have to wait, you know, 48 to 72 hours to see if it will regulate, but a lot of the times you're going to bump it 20% and the CPA is going to stay exactly the same. You could bump it another 20%. So like if you had a campaign doing well at $100 a day, you could bump 20% a day, see how the CPA holds, and if you've done it five or six times and the CPA has generally stayed the same, you can get more aggressive. So you can literally go from let's say 240 a day to 340 a day, then 500 a day, then 750 a day, then 1,000 a day, and then you could start to really get aggressive. You could go from 1,000 to 2,000 a day to 4,000 a day, 5,000 a day, and we, as you make more and more jumps and you're giving Google a lot more data, you can make these bigger jumps in the budget as long as the CPA is actually holding. So that's how we scale the budget on a lot of these, you know, custom intent in market campaigns. We're starting small, we're boosting it up. If the CPA is actually holding, we're getting more

## [51:42 - 54:48]

and more aggressive. If the CPA, let's say we go from, you know, $5,000 a day to $7,500 a day, and the CPA blows up, we're going to gradually back it back down. So you might go from, if you went to $7,500 a day and the CPA went up 50%, you're going to probably drop it $500. Then you might drop it another $500 and drop it back down until you can find a good point where you can hit your target CPA again. The whole time you're bumping up budgets, you're going into the demographic section and you're turning off ages, income levels, genders, you know, things that aren't hitting CPA. So the campaign is going to get stronger and stronger and quicker as you feed more budget into it. And after we find a winning offer in campaigns, we're using Google Analytics to find the best audiences to try out next. Like I said, we are going into the audience section in Google Analytics, and we're finding in-market audiences, affinity audiences that we can scale up. Okay, we're going very, very deep into that. And then from there, we will actually drill down into certain demos. So let's say you have your data saying males 35 to 45 for in-market for financial services, it's doing very well for you in a broader campaign. So you might have a broader campaign that's 25 to 65 plus in-market, it's very broad, it's doing very well. You might drill down into a certain segment of that market, and you're going to launch that as a singular campaign now and see how that actually does at a higher spend. So you might have that big campaign spending $10,000 a day, and then you're going to break off a piece of that demo, and you're going to spend $1,000 a day on that smaller demo where it's males 35 to 44. And generally, we're scaling to these types of spends with two to three very strong creatives. Like I said at the beginning, the difference between like a great creative and an average creative, it's literally night and day. If you get a very, very strong creative, you can scale to the 10, 20, $30,000 a day mark. If it's not that great, the CTR is going to fall off, and you're going to run into problems with the CPA, and the CPA is going to go out of whack. So usually it's two to three very strong creatives that are taking us to these levels. And these creatives are really going to be the backbone of all the different audiences we scale out to in market, custom intent, custom infinity, all of those.
